verbatim_text: And then in part two I'll talk about user models and describe how we can build external scaffolds to improve user adaptability. And finally in the third part I'll talk about self models and introduce training objectives to improve AI systems abilities to explain themselves. Okay so let's start with the first part which is world models. But before we dive in let's just go over the basics of language models. At a high level language models are trained to predict the next token from previous contexts. So given a context like Janet gave Jack the book later the book was lost by dot dot dot the language model should predict Jack and not Janet. So how do language models learn to do this? Well at the beginning of my PhD so this was around 2020 one of the dominant narratives was that language models were simply memorizing these various features of language. So this is like the bag of heuristics view I presented earlier. So people would write papers listing out all these heuristics that language models would use to do tasks like predicting entailment. And of course early language models did rely heavily on these heuristics which were also why they were so brittle. So going back to our example maybe in its training data the language model has seen a lot of examples of text of form A gave B an object and then the indirect object B occurring again shortly after. So maybe it's just learned to copy this as a general rule. So in this case in our example it works super well the LM would correctly predict Jack as the next word.
